intent: Add Apple design system as a 4th color scheme (.apple CSS class) with full token blocks, component radius refactors, grain suppression, settings UI, and test coverage.
reviewers: kieran-typescript (conditional — TypeScript components, hooks, and types modified)

findings.title: input-group.tsx uses calc(var(--radius) - 5px) — shrinks in Apple scheme
findings.suggested_fix: Accept 6px (Apple tight aesthetic) or add component-scoped --input-group-radius hook. Document disposition.
findings.why_it_matters: The Apple scheme overrides --radius from ~14px (calc-derived) to 11px. Three usages of calc(var(--radius) - 5px) in input-group.tsx drop from ~9px to 6px. While likely acceptable for Apple's tighter inline aesthetic, this was not surfaced in the plan's scope.

findings.title: Plan-required --radius audit not performed
findings.suggested_fix: Document disposition of the 3 input-group.tsx matches found by grep. Accept or add component hooks.
findings.why_it_matters: The plan's Unit 2 verification explicitly requires: 'Run grep -r var(--radius) src/ to identify components referencing the base --radius token directly. Each must be evaluated.' This was not completed. Three matches exist in input-group.tsx.
evidence: Plan document lines 188-191 (Unit 2 Verification section): '--radius audit' requirement

findings.title: ColorSchemePicker preview colors are hardcoded hex values
findings.why_it_matters: The schemePreviewColors duplicate token values from theme.css and can drift independently. Pre-existing pattern inherited from Clean/Professional/Vibrant implementations — not a regression.
evidence: schemePreviewColors object lines 7-35 uses hardcoded hex like '#ffffff', '#f5f5f7', '#0066cc'
Same pattern existed for professional, vibrant, and clean before this change

preExisting.title: loadPersistedPrefs invalid scheme fallback defaults to 'professional' not 'clean'
preExisting.suggested_fix: Change 'professional' fallback on line 67 to defaults.colorScheme (which is now 'clean')
preExisting.why_it_matters: When persisted localStorage has an invalid/missing colorScheme, the fallback returns 'professional' instead of the app-wide default 'clean'. This means corrupted localStorage results in Professional theme instead of Clean.
evidence: settings.ts defaults.colorScheme is also 'clean' at line 97

residualRisks: Apple dark mode WCAG AA contrast not verified in this review (4.5:1 for --foreground vs --background/card)
Visual regression from 17px base font size (~6.25% rem-scale increase) not checked — plan requires spot-checking 7 routes at desktop and mobile
motion-safe:active:scale press effect may interact with existing button animation transitions; verify no double-animation on Apple-scheme buttons
testingGaps: No test verifies non-Apple schemes produce identical visual output before/after the radius refactor (CSS variable defaults should match hardcoded values — verify with browser rendering)
No E2E test verifies the .apple class is applied when selected via Settings UI
No test for the store default migration ('professional' -> 'clean') — existing store tests assert the new default but don't explicitly test the migration path

learnings.relevance: Additive token pattern: define in :root first with current-behavior defaults, then override in scheme blocks. Directly followed by this implementation (component hooks in :root, overridden in .apple).
learnings.key_insight: The additive migration pattern ensures Professional/Vibrant/Clean are visually unchanged while Apple gets custom overrides.

coverage.suppressedDetails: P3 finding: ColorSchemePicker as ColorScheme type assertion (line 51) — suppressed as borderline pedantic; value is constrained by RadioGroup options.
coverage.tests: 49/49 unit tests pass. Build passes. TypeScript compiles cleanly. Lint: 0 errors, 327 warnings (all pre-existing).
